rows.notes: Top-centre page title. Wording is worn at the start but reads plausibly as 'Annavagia Anni 1700.' Letters are old‑style; some ambiguity in first word (could be 'Annuagia'/'Annuaia'), chosen reading based on letter shapes.
Description legible. Numeric cluster to the far right aligned with this entry reads '02 15 09' (pounds 2, shillings 15, pence 9). Vertical guide lines are clear here and amounts obey currency bounds.

Handwriting of name fairly clear as 'Calcutt pro Mic'. Rightside digits are a clustered set below the previous line; interpreted as '01 09 07'. The pence digit is slightly blotched so 7 is the most plausible reading.
Description reads 'Bampton pro anno'. Aligned numeric set read as '01 09 02'. Ink a little faint on the pence; 2 chosen based on alignment with the pence column.
Long description 'Montagu pro Tridennio & Dimidio' is clear. Numbers aligned to the right read as '01 09 06'. Slight ink blur on the pence but 6 fits alignment and spacing.
Surname 'Hughes pro anno.' clearly readable. Right column shows '00 15 00' (15 shillings). Pounds column is blank/zero; shillings and pence align well.
Entry reads 'Greenway pro Mic.' Numeric cluster read as '00 08 00' (8s). Clear separation between columns and digits; good alignment supports reading.
'Walker pro Mic' clear. Corresponding numbers read '00 10 00' (10s). Digits crisp and column alignment unambiguous.
Text reads 'Mynne pro Quadrinnio' (the Latin ordinal is faint but visible). Right-side numbers interpreted as '00 08 00'. Ink is lighter here; reading is plausible but not crisp.
Clear place name 'Leadon Hall pro anno'. Far-right shows '04 13 00' distinctly (pounds 4, shillings 13, pence 0). Good alignment and legible digits.
'Broom pro Mic' readable. Right column printed as '00 10 04' (10s 4d). Slight hairline over pence but digits clear.

Left part shows a brace with 'Tintinhull {' and 'Rush pro Sesquianno' on same vertical group. The numeric cluster aligned to the first of this pair reads '02 06 00'. The brace grouping makes the textual reading slightly ambiguous; numerical alignment is moderate.
Second name in the brace group reads 'Hopkins pro Sesquianno'. The large numeric to the right reads '07 08 00' — digits are bold and well aligned. It is larger than neighboring sums and is legible.
Entry reads 'Cliffton Henry pro Mic:'. Right-hand figures read as '01 03 09'. The pence digit is slightly slanted but clearly a 9 based on stroke formation.
'Bensington pro Mic' (sometimes spelled 'Bensington') is legible. Right-hand numbers read '02 04 04'. All digits clear and within currency bounds.
Text reads 'Trethwin before the half' (some words abbreviated). Numeric set aligned reads '01 15 11'; the pence '11' is slightly crowded and the tens-stroke less distinct, so moderate confidence.

rows.description: Trelchovin pro tridennio & dimidio recd in the 18th
rows.notes: Long line 'Trelchovin pro tridennio & dimidio recd in the 18th' (some contraction). The large right-hand figure '21 00 00' is very clear and stands out; reading for pounds 21 is confident.
Reads 'Merton Vic: pro Sesquianno.' Right-hand numbers read '07 04 00'. Alignment and digit shapes are clear; small dot after 'Sesquianno' noted but not affecting numeric reading.

rows.description: Southknrington Tenement pro anno of weth 3. & 4. due ???
rows.notes: Description is partially abbreviated/faded ('Southknrington Tenment' and a note 'pro anno of wth 3. & 4. due ...'). Right-hand numeric cluster read as '01 14 06'. The descriptive text uncertainty lowers confidence; numbers align but are moderately faint.

rows.description: Gentlemen Coroners Mr Martyn & Mr Harris
rows.notes: Line beginning 'Gentlemen Coroners' with names 'Mr Martyn & Mr Harris' is readable. There is a right-hand figure '16 00 00' aligned with this line — interpreted as pounds 16. The heading nature is borderline, but since an amount is present it is classed as an entry.

rows.description: Computus suprascriptus examinatus & approbatus per nos
rows.notes: Large script near the lower portion of the page. This is clearly a concluding/approval statement (header), no amounts present.
First signature line following the approval statement. Handwritten initials/abbreviations are somewhat stylised; read as 'Gu: Paynter R V' though letters after the colon are partly ambiguous.
Signature looks like 'Jo: Cooke subdr' (or 'subdr:'), readable but with abbreviation. No amount present.
Signature line reads 'Ric: Hurkins' (Richard Hurkins or similar). Letters are fairly legible in this signature block.
Clear signature 'Sam: Adams'. No amounts.
Signature appears as 'Dan: Ofborn' (or 'Dan: Ofborrn'/'Oborn'); spelling ambiguous because of stylised capitals and a flourish. No numeric data.
Final signature reads 'Rob: Rowe, Dec:' (the 'Dec:' likely an abbreviation of a title). Legible letters and no amounts present.
